title: PCR
steps: Centrifuge the tubes with the sequences for one minute at 3,000 g x m in the centrifuge
Centrifuge for 5 seconds in the microcentrifuge
Add nuclease-free water needed to have a final concentration of 10 ng/uL.
Vortex, followed by incubating for 20 minutes at 50°C.
Mix briefly by vortex and centrifuge for 8 seconds in mini centrifuge.
Add nuclease-free water to the first forward and reverse until a concentration of 100 uM is reached, centrifuged quickly in microcentrifuge.
Make dilutions with 10 uL of primers and 90 uL of nuclease-free water to reach a concentration of 10 uM
In 0.2 mL Eppendorf tubes make the following mixture.

A B 1 Nuclease Free Water 17 uL 2 Buffer 10X PCR 5 uL 3 50 mM MgCl2 1.5 uL 4 10 mM dNTP’s 1 uL 5 10 uM Forward primer 2.5 uL 6 10 uM Reverse primer 2.5 uL 7 DNA (25 mg/uL) 20 uL 8 Taq Platinum Polymerase 0.5 uL 9 Final Volume 50 uL

Perform the next cycle in thermocycler A B C D 1 1 Cycle Initial activation 2 minutes 94ºC 2 35 Cycles Denaturation 30 seconds 94ºC 3 35 Cycles Alignment 30 seconds 50ºC 4 35 Cycles Extension 2 minutes 72ºC 5 1 Cycle Final Extension 5 minutes 72ºC 6 Maintenance Maintenance - 4ºC
creator: Elena Elizabeth Mercado Flores
